evals.name: vague-test-the-app
evals.prompt: Can you test the app on Android and let me know if the home screen looks OK after my last change?
evals.expected_output: Agent runs preflight (check-devices.sh, flutter --version), discovers the run command from CLAUDE.md/README (including any mandatory --target / --dart-define flags), launches via launch.sh in the background, waits for the 'Flutter run key commands' banner plus the first frame, captures a screenshot via snap.sh, reads the PNG + run log, and reports a concrete verdict referencing the screenshot path.

evals.name: multi-entry-target
evals.prompt: Run my Flutter app on the emulator — note it has separate Android and iOS entry points.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes a multi-entry app, reads references/flutter.md, finds the correct --target (e.g. lib/main_android.dart) from the project docs rather than running bare `flutter run` (which may throw by design), pins -d <id>, waits for the banner, screenshots, and verdicts. Does not give up before a cold Gradle build finishes (poll, don't fixed-sleep).

evals.name: figma-comparison-optional
evals.prompt: I redesigned the capture screen to match this Figma file: https://www.figma.com/design/ABC123/App?node-id=1-2 — does the actual Android UI match?
evals.expected_output: Agent launches the app, navigates to the screen, screenshots it, and (only because a Figma URL was given) follows references/figma-comparison.md: fetches the node via the Figma MCP tool, reads both PNGs, and compares layout/color/typography/spacing by relative proportions, not pixels. If no Figma MCP server is configured, it says so and falls back to verifying against the stated intent.

evals.name: ambiguous-see-changes
evals.expected_output: Short ambiguous prompt — agent infers 'run the app and report what's visible', goes through the capture loop, and produces a verdict. If it can't tell which screen 'changes' refers to, it screenshots the entry screen, describes it, and asks the user to specify.
